items.headline: City launching business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: Cupertino is rolling out an amnesty program to help businesses get compliant with licensing requirements, combined with outreach to shopping centers. This could affect local retailers and small business owners who may have unresolved licensing issues.

items.headline: New economic incentives being offered to retail and startup businesses
items.summary: The city council approved new incentive programs designed to attract and support retail shops and startups in Cupertino. This could influence where businesses choose to locate and what support they receive.

items.headline: New mixed-use residential development approved for East Middlefield Road
items.summary: Mountain View City Council approved a development agreement for 490 East Middlefield Road on the second reading at the March 24 meeting. This mixed-use project will add residential units to the area.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for Round 4 of California's Prohousing Incentive Program at the March 24 meeting. This state grant program supports cities that remove barriers to housing production.

items.headline: City seeking public input on how to spend federal housing grants
items.summary: Santa Clara is holding a public hearing on March 24 to gather community feedback on its draft plan for spending HUD federal housing and urban development funds for the 2026/27 fiscal year. This is a chance for residents to weigh in on priorities for affordable housing and related programs.

items.headline: City holding community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale is hosting a public meeting on Monday, March 23 to discuss a safe parking initiative. This appears to be a program addressing vehicle dwelling or overnight parking issues in the city.
